Read our methodology below and give us a fair […]

The post Best Residential Proxies for Web Scraping in 2026 (With Real Test Data) appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
Residential Proxies for Web Scraping: 7 Providers Tested

Disclosure: Our team at Ziny did this testing. Obviously we think Ziny has the best value for most use cases, but we’ve tried to be objective in presenting our competitors and showing the raw data from real tests. Read our methodology below and give us a fair shake by testing out for yourself with a free trial.

Ever tried scraping Amazon or LinkedIn recently? Get blocked after 50 requests? Don’t feel bad. Websites these days are insanely good at identifying and blocking bots, their anti-bot measures detect datacenter IPs instantly.

If you are new to proxies, check out our complete guide to residential proxies to understand the basics. The solution? Residential proxies rotate your scrapers through real home IP addresses

We put Ziny head-to-head against 6 major competitors by scraping a total of 100,000 pages from Amazon, Google, LinkedIn, Instagram, and Zillow. In this post, we will show you the results from these tests, with real performance numbers, pricing comparisons, and candid assessments of when and how each provider excels.

The Bottom Fell Out of Datacenter Proxies

Once upon a time, datacenter proxies worked just fine for scraping most sites. No more. For scraping any protected websites, they won’t work.

Here’s what happens the moment your datacenter IPs touch a modern protected website:

  • Instant blocks – Amazon, LinkedIn, Instagram throw datacenter IPs on the banned list after 20-50 requests
  • CAPTCHA hell – Anti-bot systems flag your request within a second and force you to solve endless CAPTCHAs
  • Fake data – Sites detect your requests as a bot and respond with false/incomplete data making your entire crawl pointless
  • Constant troubleshooting – You have to constantly rotate your proxies manually, troubleshoot bans, and watch your scraper repeatedly fail

Residential proxies address this fundamental problem by rotating through real household IPs from actual ISPs. To the websites you’re scraping, your traffic looks exactly like someone just browsing from their home. This legitimacy is what makes it finally possible to scrape at scale on modern websites.

Residential Proxies Explained: What are they and Why You Need Them in 2026

Residential proxies are IP addresses tied to actual residential customer addresses by Internet Service Providers. Unlike datacenter IPs that come from servers, datacenters, etc. and are obviously fake, residential IPs are tied to real households, that is why anti-bot systems have a hard time detecting them.

The Technical Detail

When websites use device fingerprinting, combining signals like your IP, user agent, browser signatures, and access patterns, residential proxies are the key to making your traffic look legitimate. They are not on any bot-tracking databases because to ISPs and network providers they’re real consumer connections.

The Practical Trade-off

Residential proxies rotate your traffic through real people’s devices (computers, phones, tablets), so they’re more expensive and a little less stable than datacenter options. Most providers charge by bandwidth usage which can be difficult to budget for in larger projects if you’re not careful.

Our Testing Methodology

In order to make the comparisons as fair as possible, we took a systematic approach for testing each of the 7 providers in our comparison:

We Scraped

  • 100,000 total page requests (broken into high-protection targets)
  • Amazon (30,000 pages): Product listings, reviews, pricing
  • LinkedIn (25,000 pages): Profile pages, company data, jobs
  • Instagram (20,000 pages): Public profiles, posts, engagement data
  • Google (15,000 pages): Search results, business listings
  • Zillow (10,000 pages): Property listings across 20 cities

We Measured Success by

  • Success rate = % of requests that returned the target data without blocks, CAPTCHAs, errors
  • Speed = Avg response time from request sent to data received
  • Cost = Total spent to scrape 100,000 pages according to standard pricing tiers

Consistency Check

  • Same scraping scripts used across all providers
  • Same target pages scraped in parallel
  • Same time windows to control for day-part/site-specific fluctuations
  • Multiple test runs to ensure repeatability

Download Our Data: You can find our full testing spreadsheet with raw results, timestamps, and more in the download link at the bottom of the post

The 7 Providers We Tested: Complete Results

We scraped the same set of protected sites including Amazon, Google, LinkedIn, Instagram, and Zillow with each provider. Here’s our full results:

1. Ziny – Our Residential Proxy Solution for High-Volume Scraping

Full Disclosure: This is our product. We build it, use it, and now you’re getting a free tour.

Test Results: 99.2% success | 1.8s speed | $39 per 100K pages

What Makes Us Different

We have 30M+ IPs in 195 countries providing fresh addresses with city-level targeting. The unlimited bandwidth plans let you avoid surprise bills, scraping 100K pages cost $39 flat vs $125-$387 with most competitors.

We’re consistently bypassing Amazon, LinkedIn anti-bot systems with success rates above 99%. Residential, mobile, ISP, and datacenter proxies are all included in one plan, so you can switch proxy types based on target difficulty without having to maintain multiple subscriptions.

Perfect For: E-commerce data collection, social media scraping, market research, agencies with multiple clients/scrapers

Pricing: Pay-as-you-go $0.40/GB, Unlimited plans from $99/month

→ Try It Free: 7 Day Free Trial

2. Bright Data – The Traditional Enterprise Option

Test Results: 98.7% success | 2.1s speed | $125 per 100K pages

Bright Data’s 72M+ IPs includes coordinate-level targeting which is even more granular than city precision. Proxy Manager gives you precise control with rule-based proxy routing for complex use cases.

The Trade-off: At $12.50/GB you can quickly rack up high costs. The dashboard takes time to learn, but is worth it for maximum control.

Best For: Enterprise Fortune 500 companies, complex multi-site scraping workflows, teams that need maximum control

3. Oxylabs – Premium Reliability Brand

Test Results: 98.9% success | 1.9s speed | $155 per 100K pages

Oxylabs’ 102M+ IPs delivered extremely consistent performance throughout our testing. ISO/IEC 27001:2022 certification and white-glove support justifies the premium price tag.

Their Scraper API handles the parsing and extraction heavy lifting, significantly speeding up development. They also source their proxies ethically and transparently with user consent, which is increasingly important in the industry.

The Trade-off: $7.75/GB pricing with $100 minimum monthly commitment. Premium priced for a reason.

Best For: Enterprises in regulated industries, teams working with sensitive data, businesses that prioritize ethical proxy sourcing

4. NetNut – ISP Residential Proxy Provider with Focus on Speed

Test Results: 97.8% success | 1.6s speed | $110 per 100K pages

NetNut’s direct relationships with ISPs result in the fastest response times we tested. The 85M+ ISP proxies are a blend of residential legitimacy and datacenter-like speed and stability.

Session stickiness persists the same IP for 24 hours, great for workflows where you need to be logged in and navigate a multi-step process.

The Trade-off: Limited mobile proxy offerings and $100 minimum monthly commitment. Speed comes at a cost in some anti-bot bypass scenarios.

Best For: Real-time pricing monitoring, high-frequency scraping projects, workflows that need very stable sessions

5. Smartproxy – Mid-Tier Residential Provider With Great Usability

Test Results: 96.4% success | 2.3s speed | $95 per 100K pages

Smartproxy’s 55M+ IP network provides solid performance with super user-friendly setup. Their dashboard is designed for beginners and lets you start scraping in minutes. Documentation is clear and their support is responsive 24/7 which helps teams that don’t have dedicated DevOps.

The Trade-off: Success rates were the lowest on LinkedIn (94.2% vs 99+% from top performers). Scaling plans up quickly in the pricing tiers.

Best For: Small agencies, developers new to residential proxies, medium-volume projects

6. SOAX – Flexible Residential Provider With Ethical Proxies

Test Results: 96.1% success | 2.4s speed | $88 per 100K pages

SOAX’s 155M+ proxy network has a mix of residential, mobile, ISP, and datacenter—giving you access to all the types in every plan. Strong mobile proxy coverage across 190+ countries.

Their ethical sourcing policy collects explicit user consent before proxies go to market. ASN targeting lets you get super granular with ISP-level precision for advanced scraping strategies.

The Trade-off: Occasional throttling during high-demand periods. Success rate is lowest of the providers on our heaviest anti-bot targets.

Best For: Teams that put a premium on ethical sourcing, scraping mobile devices, projects that need all types of proxies

7. Webshare – Static Residential Proxy Provider

Test Results: 93.7% success | 2.2s speed | $230 per 100K pages

Webshare specializes in static residential proxies, ones that never rotate. They only have 1,000 static IPs, and those don’t rotate for weeks or months.

Setup is amazingly simple for workflows where you need the same IP for weeks/months at a time e.g. account management, multi-step forms.

The Trade-off: Webshare’s static IPs have the lowest success rates on protected sites (93.7% best case). You pay a lot more per page than Ziny ($230 vs $39-155) and you’re stuck with a small 1,000 IP pool.

Best For: Account management use cases, session-based scraping requiring IP persistence, specialized static IP workflows

The Lowdown in a Nutshell

Budget Under $100?

SOAX at $88 or Smartproxy at $95 per 100K pages. Both have good docs and support for people just getting started with scraping.

Scraping Protected Sites (Amazon/LinkedIn/Instagram)?

Ziny at 99.2% success or Oxylabs at 98.9% success. Success rates >99% beat Amazon’s anti-bot by a mile. Oxylabs performance on LinkedIn was a close second.

Need Maximum Speed?

NetNut at 1.6s avg response time had the best speed and response time of any provider we tested. The direct ISP connections seem to be the difference.

Enterprise with Complex Requirements?

Bright Data or Oxylabs. We see most of our enterprise customers landing on these two for maximum control, advanced routing rules, or dedicated account management.

Scraping 100K+ Pages Per Month?

Ziny’s unlimited bandwidth plans are key. The $99 Unlimited plan eliminated surprise costs and provided by far the best economics at scale.

Ethical Proxy Sourcing a Priority?

Oxylabs with ISO certification or SOAX with transparent consent info emphasized ethical proxy acquisition the most.

Need Static IPs?

Webshare had the best performance and functionality for specialized static residential IPs.

Real Scraping Examples: Performance Comparison

E-Commerce Price Monitoring

Target: Amazon product pages (30,000 pages)

Winner: Ziny – 99.4% success rate

Cost Comparison:

  • Ziny (unlimited bandwidth): $39
  • Oxylabs: $190
  • Bright Data: $153

Winner Take All: Why Ziny Won Scraping Amazon

Amazon is the gold standard for scraping today. They have some of the most sophisticated anti-bot systems on the web, behavioral analysis, device fingerprinting, request pattern analysis, and more. Ziny’s success rate stayed above 99% even when scraping Amazon for 12-16 hours in a row. The unlimited bandwidth plan also provided essential cost certainty, Amazon pages are large with lots of images, product HTML and CSS which get expensive under a pay-per-GB plan.

Key Learning: For sustained scraping on Amazon, only choose a provider with a proven success rate >99%. The difference between 96% success and 99% success is the difference between 4,000 failed requests and 1,000 failed requests per 100K pages. Fail that many requests and the time & resources you waste quickly adds up.

LinkedIn Scraping

Target: LinkedIn professional profiles (25,000 pages)

Winner: Oxylabs – 99.1% success rate

Winner Take All: Why Oxylabs Won LinkedIn Scraping

LinkedIn has the most aggressive bot detection including real-time unusual behavioral analysis. Oxylabs’ session handling & intelligent request retry logic outperformed all other providers when it came to bypassing these protections. The Scraper API also took a load off of extraction, especially for structured profile data like work history.

Cost Comparison:

  • Oxylabs: $194
  • Ziny: $39 (unlimited bandwidth)
  • Bright Data: $156

While Oxylabs achieved the highest LinkedIn scraping success rate, Ziny’s 98.8% was nearly equivalent but at 80% lower cost for high-volume scraping.

Key Learning: Scraping LinkedIn requires a lot of patience. Making too many requests per second/site triggers instant bans no matter how good your proxies are. Successful scraping used 3-5 second delays between requests with some intelligent session management.

Real Estate Data Collection

Target: Zillow home listings in 20+ cities (10,000 pages)

Winner: Ziny – Perfect for local target markets

Winner Take All: Why Ziny Won Real Estate Scraping

Real estate data collection at scale requires precise geo-targeting. Want to collect listings for a Boston realtor? You need Boston IPs. Miami agent? Miami IPs. Ziny has the best coverage of any provider we tested for geo-specific scraping, city-level targeting in 195 countries.

Provider Comparison:

  • Ziny: City-level in 195+ countries
  • Oxylabs: Coordinate-level (even more granular, but at higher cost)
  • Smartproxy: City-level in major markets
  • NetNut: State/region-level in most regions

Key Learning: For geo-specific scraping, make sure your proxy provider has actual coverage in your target markets before you sign up. “Coverage in 195 countries” doesn’t matter if you live and need proxies for Des Moines, Iowa and your provider only has state-level targeting.

A Large Scale Research Project

Target: 500,000+ product reviews across e-commerce sites

Winner: Ziny – Unlimited bandwidth

Cost Comparison:

  • Ziny: Unlimited bandwidth plan $199/month = $199
  • Pay-per-GB competitors: 2TB bandwidth @ avg. $1.94/GB = $3,875
  • Saving: $3,676 (94.9% less cost)

Winner Take All: Why Ziny Won Cost at Scale

The bandwidth costs get astronomical once you exceed 100GB per month with providers that charge per GB. Ziny’s unlimited bandwidth plan let us scrap on an all-you-can-eat basis. We could focus on optimization for data quality rather than constantly monitoring our GB usage.

Key Learning: Unlimited bandwidth plans are a no-brainer for projects expected to exceed 100GB per month of bandwidth usage. This depends on each provider’s offering and where your cutover point falls. We hit it at about 300GB per month total bandwidth usage.

Ready to Give Scraping Without Tears a Try?

The right residential proxy provider can make scraping stop being a constant tech support nightmare and finally be a reliable, scalable operation.

After putting 7 providers through their paces on 100,000 requests, we think Ziny has the best combination of performance, reliability, and value for most scraping projects. We’re honest about where we fall short and invite you to do your own testing to see what works for your specific needs.

Give Ziny a Free 7-Day Trial

  • No CC required
  • Full access to all features & proxy types
  • Test out your actual scraping targets
  • Get a refund if we don’t outperform your existing solution
  • Migration help from our team if you want to switch

→ Try It Free for 7 Days

Questions? Need More Help?

  • Send us a note: support@ziny.io
  • Chat live: Click “chat” on our site
  • Book a call: 15-minute demo call
  • Docs: Read our FAQ

We stand behind our testing, methods, and results with full transparency. If you are seeing different results, we want to know.

Ultimately your choice comes down to the unique specifics of your project. Protection level of your targets, total volume, budget, technical skill you have available etc. For us Ziny was the right choice, but we don’t expect every scraper to end up here. Make your decision based on testing with your real targets, not marketing copy.

Happy scraping!

The post Best Residential Proxies for Web Scraping in 2026 (With Real Test Data) appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: What Is a Subnet Mask?
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
How Subnet Masks Keep Networks Organized Networks can’t run without structure. Every connection relies on a defined route, and every route requires direction. That direction comes from the subnet mask. A subnet mask tells each connected device which portion of an IP address represents the local network, and which part identifies the device itself. Without […]

The post What Is a Subnet Mask? appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
How Subnet Masks Keep Networks Organized

Networks can’t run without structure. Every connection relies on a defined route, and every route requires direction. That direction comes from the subnet mask. A subnet mask tells each connected device which portion of an IP address represents the local network, and which part identifies the device itself.

Without subnet masks, each device on a network would blindly transmit data to every other device. Nothing would know where to send or receive information, and networks would grind to a halt under constant collisions.

A subnet mask solves this problem. It clearly demarcates where each segment of the internet begins and ends. Communication routes follow defined lanes of information instead of spraying data in random bursts of noise.

Proxies like Ziny Proxy build on this same logic. Their entire infrastructure is organised around subnet rules, where separate IP ranges are cleanly split across different proxy pools. Each residential proxy subnet is optimised for speed and stability. The result is maximum efficiency, less congestion, and consistent performance across the board.

What a Subnet Mask Is

A subnet mask is a 32-bit number that’s assigned to an IP address. It breaks up the IP address into tA subnet mask is a 32-bit number assigned to an IP address. It splits the IP address into two sections: a network section and a host section.

Put simply, it’s the identifier that labels which devices are on the local subnet and which aren’t.

You might also see this referred to as a netmask. The terms are interchangeable and refer to the same value.

A common subnet mask is 255.255.255.0. This is represented by:

  • Three of four 8-bit numbers (octets) representing the network portion
  • One octet representing the host portion

Ziny Proxy uses this same methodology to manage its data centres. Each subnet mask precisely delineates where one IP pool begins and ends, preventing user requests from overlapping or bleeding into another subnet, keeping proxy traffic clean and organised rather than tangled.

How Subnet Mask Works

When an IP address is combined with its subnet mask, the binary digits assigned the value 1 identify network bits, and the digits assigned the value 0 identify host bits.

  • If two or more devices share the same network bits, then they are on the same subnet and can communicate directly with each other.
  • If they have different network bits, then data must be sent through a router or gateway.

The same concept holds true for router subnet masks. They use the same process to determine if routers forward traffic internally within the local network or externally to other segments.

Ziny Proxy mirrors this internal logic when routing user IPs through its network. Each node is aware of the subnets in play, so connections never overlap, routing stays predictable, and performance remains stable. This is part of why choosing the right proxy setup matters as much as the underlying network architecture.

Subnetting and Mask Sizes

Subnetting is the process of dividing one large network into multiple smaller subnets. This saves IP space and optimizes performance.

Here are some examples of common subnet mask sizes:

  • /24 (255.255.255.0) — 256 total IP addresses
  • /26 (255.255.255.192) — 62 total usable hosts
  • 255.255.252.0 subnet (/22) — 1022 total usable hosts

Each step increment changes the number of hosts a subnet can accommodate.

Ziny Proxy applies the same subnetting principles when allocating IP addresses across its network. By fine-tuning subnet sizes, it avoids congestion and ensures proxy nodes perform evenly even under peak demand — something particularly important for high-volume use cases like web scraping at scale.

Why Subnet Masks Matter

Without subnet masks, every device on a network would blast data to every other device on the network. This kind of noise would quickly bring the internet to a standstill.

Subnet masks create:

  • Clear boundaries between traffic segments
  • Limited collisions
  • Enforced security boundaries

Precision in subnet mapping is even more important for proxy services like Ziny Proxy. It ensures that every IP range is properly mapped out, which helps prevent IP leaks, routing errors, and creates consistent, predictable traffic patterns across data centers and regions.

A clean subnet architecture is the underlying reason Ziny Proxy is so reliable, even with thousands of simultaneous connections.

Default Masks and Common Uses

Networks used to use class-based subnet masks before CIDR notation was a thing:

  • Class A: 255.0.0.0
  • Class B: 255.255.0.0
  • Class C: 255.255.255.0

Nowadays, most modern systems use CIDR notation instead.

The most common subnet mask for home or small office routers is /24 (255.255.255.0). This supports up to 254 usable devices — more than enough for most small networks.

If you’re wondering what is the subnet mask for /30, then it’s 255.255.255.252. This subnet only allows two usable IP addresses and is usually reserved for point-to-point connections.

Ziny Proxy’s backend also runs on this same underlying principle. Every subnet is isolated yet reachable, similar to having private lanes on a heavily controlled highway.

How to Find Subnet Mask of an IP Address

If you’ve ever wondered how to find subnet mask of an IP address, here’s how:

  • Windows: open Command Prompt → type ipconfig
  • macOS / Linux: open Terminal → type ifconfig or ip a

You’ll see your subnet mask listed next to your IP address.

It’s also usually displayed on your router’s web configuration page, under the label “Subnet Mask” or “Netmask.”

Ziny Proxy automates this validation process on its backend when spinning up new IPs. It scans each IP block and subnet mask before assigning it, ensuring no overlaps or invalid entries make it through. If you’ve ever run into routing issues with a proxy, our proxy errors troubleshooting guide covers the most common causes and exact fixes.

Common Problems of Subnet Mask

A valid subnet mask must have:

  • All 1s grouped together in binary form
  • All 0s grouped together

Example of a valid subnet mask: 255.255.255.0
Example of an invalid subnet mask: 255.0.255.0

Invalid or overlapping subnet masks lead to:

  • Devices getting dropped from networks
  • Data looping back and forth

Ziny Proxy prevents this from happening by validating every assigned IP address and subnet mask before use. It flags any potential routing conflicts and rejects any entries that don’t cleanly fit into its subnet architecture. This ensures every proxy address functions cleanly and predictably, regardless of which server is in use.

Simple Examples of Subnet Mask

  • Example 1:
    IP: 192.168.1.50
    Mask: 255.255.255.0
    Range: 192.168.1.1–192.168.1.254
  • Example 2:
    IP: 10.0.0.130
    Mask: 255.255.255.192 (/26)
    Usable hosts: 62
  • Example 3:
    IP: 172.16.8.10
    Mask: 255.255.252.0 (/22)
    Usable hosts: 1022

Ziny Proxy also uses similar calculation and verification processes when provisioning IPs across its residential proxy pools. Each subnet is stress-tested and balanced so users always get even speeds, low latency, and zero overlaps.

In Conclusion

A subnet mask separates an IP address into a network part and host part. It makes everything clean, logical, and traceable.

Every network uses them. Every reliable proxy system is built on them.

Ziny Proxy builds its global IP management capabilities on this very foundation. Each subnet is isolated, finely-tuned, and carefully verified to deliver stable performance.

From the 255.255.255.0 subnet you use in your home to the 255.255.252.0 subnet used in large data center infrastructure, subnet masks are what define how digital traffic flows.

They keep the internet connected, secure, and sane. Ziny Proxy just makes that process smoother and faster for everyone.

The post What Is a Subnet Mask? appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: What Is a Residential Proxy
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
Residential Proxy A residential proxy works by connecting you to the internet through an IP address that comes from a home network. Rerouting your traffic through these networks disguises your activity as being generated by a standard user on a home network rather than by a bot or datacenter server. Major benefits of using residential […]

The post What Is a Residential Proxy appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
Residential Proxy

A residential proxy works by connecting you to the internet through an IP address that comes from a home network. Rerouting your traffic through these networks disguises your activity as being generated by a standard user on a home network rather than by a bot or datacenter server.

Major benefits of using residential proxies for your workflow:

  • Accepted as legitimate traffic by websites
  • Avoid restrictions based on your physical location
  • Conduct operations without being flagged as suspicious

Residential proxies are unique because they mask your traffic as activity generated by a real household connecting to the internet. Every website you visit sees your requests as coming from a legitimate residential IP address. But instead of originating from a home computer, your traffic is coming through the residential proxy server.

Ziny Proxy specialises in residential proxies that work. Our network only provides access to genuine residential IPs that behave like standard home connections instead of recognised datacenter proxy ranges.

How Residential Proxies Work

Internet traffic is always routed through an IP address. That address tells every website you visit where you are located. Residential proxies disguise your location by routing your connection through another device’s IP address, specifically, one assigned to a home network by an ISP.

Your requests pass through that intervening device before reaching their target destination. Those home IPs are real addresses assigned to actual network devices. That means when your request arrives at sites like Google, Facebook, or Amazon, those platforms see traffic coming from a legitimate real-world IP, just like every other user.

Ziny Proxy provides access to those IPs on a rental basis. Sign up, pick a region, grab your proxy credentials, and connect through Ziny Proxy’s pool of verified IPs. Once connected, your traffic appears to sites as originating directly from those network devices.

Network stability matters just as much as privacy for most users. Whether you are scraping data, auditing advertisements, or expanding into new markets, a connection that triggers alarms will stop you before you reach your goals. Our guide on how to scrape websites without getting blocked in 2026 walks through exactly how to keep your operations running cleanly at scale.

Why Residential Proxy Detection Exists

Websites do not want spammers. They do not want malicious scrapers harvesting their content. And they certainly do not want botnets or bad actors engaging with their platforms at scale.

That is why websites use proxy detection tools. Speed and request limits are just the beginning. Most modern detection systems look for patterns of bot-like activity that indicate VPNs, proxies, or automated traffic.

Proxy ranges from datacenters are easily detected because they originate from large blocks of IP addresses owned by known providers. A residential IP, on the other hand, could belong to any individual with an internet connection, meaning there are millions of potential addresses to work through before a pattern emerges.

Ziny Proxy differentiates itself with a robust, always-growing network of residential IPs from around the world, continuously rotating connections through constantly changing network devices.

The Difference Between Datacenter and Residential Proxies

Datacenter IPs originate from server farms. Residential proxy IPs come from real home internet connections. While this makes residential IPs harder to detect, it also affects speed and availability.

Datacenter IP addresses are routinely flagged by detection systems. Some sites publish these IP blocks publicly, making it straightforward to block traffic originating from them. Real residential IPs, assigned by ISPs to real users, are far harder to block at scale, doing so would mean cutting off millions of legitimate visitors.

Connecting through a residential proxy makes your traffic far less likely to be flagged as suspicious. When you need to run long-term operations without manual resets or interruptions, residential proxies are the more reliable choice. If you are still deciding which proxy type fits your use case, the proxy selection guide for scraping, social media, and monitoring covers exactly when to use each type.

How to Use Residential Proxies

Using residential proxies is straightforward. Providers give you login credentials that look like this:

Proxy Address: 107.164.229.39
Proxy Port: 19546
Proxy Username: a7c52f1b
Proxy Password: jd64f07620

Those credentials redirect your traffic through your provider’s network. Enter them into the proxy settings of your browser, app, or tool, and you are ready to go.

Residential proxies suit almost any legitimate online task. Price monitoring, competitor research, and social media account management are just a few everyday use cases that benefit from clean residential connections.

Ziny Proxy supports all popular tools including Selenium, Puppeteer, Scrapy, and more. Proxies can be used for anything legal and within a website’s terms of service — your results depend on how well you understand the tool.

How to Set Up Residential Proxies

It only takes five steps to set up residential proxies with Ziny Proxy:

  • Sign up with Ziny Proxy
  • Choose your region, or let the system assign one automatically
  • Copy your provided IP address, port, username, and password
  • Paste credentials into your scraping tool or browser settings
  • Test the connection to confirm it is working

Ziny Proxy supports both rotating and static residential proxies. Use rotating proxies when sending many requests quickly, such as for scraping, as these automatically switch IPs at defined intervals so each request uses a different address from your pool.

Static residential proxies retain the same IP for the duration of your session. They are best suited for accessing accounts or logging into websites where a consistent identity matters.

Running into issues during setup? The complete index of 25 proxy errors and exact fixes covers every common configuration problem with step-by-step solutions.

Free Residential Proxies

Free residential proxies sound like a dream, but in most cases, they are scams. A lot of free proxies work by repurposing bandwidth from infected devices or stealing bandwidth. They are often unstable, unsafe, and risk exposing your data to sketchy networks.

Free proxies also get detected much faster. They are shared by thousands of users. Detection systems can easily flag them as suspicious.

A much better way is to take advantage of a free trial by a reliable provider. Ziny Proxy offers that for new users that would like to experience the network first before subscribing. You get to enjoy access to real residential IPs on a controlled basis without risking your privacy.

When it comes to reliability, a genuine free trial is always better than a free, shady proxy.

The Real Benefits of Residential Proxies

Access data from around the world exactly as real users see it.

Browse without borders. See how search results vary from country to country, monitor ad placements across international markets, or retrieve local pricing from competitor e-commerce sites without exposing your operations.

Our guide to price monitoring proxies for Amazon, Walmart, and Shopify is a practical example of how residential proxies power competitive research, letting you view those stores exactly as local shoppers do.

Ziny Proxy supports this with broad global IP coverage. IPs are monitored for stability so you can run long sessions without resets, and all connections come from verified residential sources.

Legal and Ethical Use

Residential proxies are not illegal. Accessing public information with a proxy, managing multiple accounts, or bypassing geographic restrictions on content you are entitled to access are all legitimate uses.

Where residential proxies become a legal concern is when they are used to access private information or overwhelm servers in ways that damage normal operations.

Ziny Proxy is an ethical provider, every IP in our network comes from users who have agreed to let us route connections through their devices. If you are unsure about web scraping rules in your country, the legal guide to web scraping in 2026 covers what is and is not permitted by region.

Practical Scenarios

Manage Facebook ads in France, check Google search results from Brazil, or see how Amazon loads in Spain, all from a single connection. Running operations through residential proxies removes the geographic and account limitations that would otherwise slow you down.

Most teams use a mix of rotating and static residential proxies to manage accounts by region. For TikTok specifically, where ban risk is high and account separation is critical, our guide on TikTok proxies for multi-account management covers the right setup in detail.

Why Ziny Proxy Stands Out

You deserve a proxy provider that just works.

You do not have to read every article on proxies to understand their value — you already know why they are useful. Ziny Proxy just makes them easier to use.

Whether you are running a lightweight setup or a large-scale operation, Ziny Proxy delivers fast, reliable, and clean IPs that perform consistently when it counts.

Got questions? Explore more articles on the Ziny Proxy blog to sharpen your results and learn more about getting the most from proxies.

Put our proxies to the test today. Sign up with Ziny Proxy and see why customers around the world trust us with their most important online operations.

Final Thoughts

Residential proxies bridge the divide between privacy and performance. They let you work as free as you want online, collect data, and work on your projects without looking suspicious. The power of these proxies lie in their authenticity, their ability to leverage real connections, instead of server ranges.

Ziny Proxy has made that concept simple. Ziny Proxy delivers residential IPs that can handle real workloads, and comes with effortless setup and excellent uptime to boot. If you are scraping, testing, or managing your regional campaigns, you want a proxy built for consistency.

Best of all, the most efficient proxies are those that do not get in your way, and that is Ziny Proxy.

The post What Is a Residential Proxy appeared first on Ziny.io.

item.title: What is Janitor AI? Full Guide + Proxies
item.dc:creator: Daniel Cole
item.description:
Janitor AI: More Than Just a Chatbot So you have messy data you need to clean or format, or want to scrape something small or run a quick test. You do not want to spin up another full scraper or go through a lengthy setup just for small tasks. You want something lightweight, free, easy […]

The post What is Janitor AI? Full Guide + Proxies appeared first on Ziny.io.
item.content:encoded:
Janitor AI: More Than Just a Chatbot

So you have messy data you need to clean or format, or want to scrape something small or run a quick test. You do not want to spin up another full scraper or go through a lengthy setup just for small tasks. You want something lightweight, free, easy to use, understands natural language, and works cleanly with proxies.

Janitor AI fits this use case surprisingly well. While many treat it as simply a chatbot platform with canned characters, that is only scratching the surface.

Janitor AI actually handles lightweight scraping and data tasks effectively once you understand how to use it. The tool underneath acts as a data formatter, cleaner, and sorter built on top of NLP. It was not built with scraping specifically in mind, but it gets the job done and delivers quick results with minimum effort.

What Janitor AI Really Is

Janitor AI is a chatbot app launched in 2023. Users can build custom AI characters and chat with them, including personality settings, scenario creation, guardrails, and example dialogues for the bot to follow.

It also works well as a quick data sorter. Since the UI is conversational, you do not need to write scripts or do anything technically complex. Just type what you want and have it format, classify, or filter raw scraped data right there. Very convenient for anyone working with scraped text data.

Uses for Janitor AI

Chat-first interactive workflow. Rather than writing scripts, you can simply instruct Janitor AI in plain English.

Scrubbing and filtering. A solid NLP tool for removing clutter, highlighting important content, or reformatting scraped text.

Generative AI. Summarise, rewrite, and structure unstructured data quickly.

Machine learning. Builds on previous responses and learns from conversation patterns over time.

API and proxy support. Connect directly to external LLMs like OpenAI or Claude, and use proxies to mask your traffic. If you are handling larger scraping operations alongside this, our guide on how to scrape websites without getting blocked in 2026 covers the broader strategy.

Why Proxies Matter for Janitor AI

Once you start connecting Janitor AI to external models, you will quickly run into rate limits, bans, and geo-restrictions. This is where a proxy becomes essential. A good proxy masks your traffic for privacy, bypasses blocks, and prevents the frustrating API errors that come with unprotected connections.

For stable setups, Ziny Proxy’s rotating residential IPs are a reliable choice — they do not trigger Cloudflare repeatedly and keep Janitor AI running without interruption. Not sure which proxy type suits your setup? The proxy selection guide for scraping, automation, and multi-login workflows breaks it down clearly.

Security and Risks

Janitor AI does not leak your chats or IP by default. Conversations are private unless you mark them as public. The biggest risk comes when connecting Janitor AI to third-party APIs. Some providers, particularly OpenAI, have strict rules around certain prompt types and can ban your account. Adding a proxy gives you an extra layer of protection and greater control over your own settings.

Why People Use Janitor AI

The appeal of Janitor AI over a direct API connection is the ability to customise beyond just your API calls and NLP model:

  • Free and paid characters
  • Integrated proxy support
  • Flexibility for data scrubbing or running projects you know would get flagged through other routes

Step By Step Guide to Configure Janitor AI with Proxy

1: Create your Janitor AI account

  1. Go to Janitor AI and sign up / log in.
  2. Verify your email (if prompted).
  3. Land on the dashboard and get familiar with the layout (characters/library, chats, settings).

2: Explore or create a character

  1. Browse existing characters and open a chat; or
  2. Create your own:
    • Name, persona, backstory, goals.
    • Add example dialogues and guardrails (what the character should/shouldn’t do).
    • Save and test a few messages.

3: Pick how you will power Janitor AI

You have two common routes:

  • Built-in/default model: No extra setup.
  • External LLM via API or proxy: More control, often better quality/latency, and model choice (e.g., GPT or Claude).

4: Connect an external model with API (no proxy yet)

  1. Open API / Model Settings inside Janitor AI.
  2. Choose your provider (e.g., OpenAI).
  3. Paste your API key from the provider.
  4. Save settings and run a quick test (send a short message to your character).

it is important to do this as it confirms your account, key, and basic setup work before adding a proxy layer. (Interface flow reported by users: “API settings” lives in the top-right menu; you can select provider options there.)

5: Decide whether you need a proxy

Use a proxy if any of these apply:

  • You are geo-blocked, throttled, or need privacy (mask IP).
  • You want a reverse-proxy bridge to an external LLM (community method to route Janitor AI traffic to models like Claude/OpenAI through one URL).

Two types at a glance

  • Forward proxy (user-side): You configure your device/browser to tunnel traffic through a proxy server for privacy/geo access.
  • Reverse proxy (middleware): A gateway URL sits between Janitor AI and the LLM API. This is handy for devs and power users (centralizes auth, rate limits, model switching).

6: Set up a forward proxy (user-side, simplest)

  1. Pick a reputable proxy provider (Ziny is a great option here).
  2. Configure the proxy:
    • Desktop: OS network proxy settings or a proxy app/extension.
    • Mobile: Use Wi-Fi HTTP proxy settings or a trusted VPN with proxy support.
  3. Confirm your IP changed (whoer.to).
  4. Open Janitor AI and chat normally.

7: Set up a reverse proxy (bridge to external LLM)

Option A is to use a community/hosted reverse proxy (easiest):

  1. Get your proxy URL from the hosted service (it routes to the LLM for you).
  2. In Janitor AI, go to API settings then Proxy/Custom model (wording varies).
  3. Choose Custom/Proxy and paste the Other API/Proxy URL.
  4. Paste your API key (if the service provides one) in the key field.
  5. Save, refresh, and Check API or send a test message.

Option B is to Host your own reverse proxy (developer):

  1. Spin up Nginx/Caddy (or a tiny Node/Python gateway) on a server.
  2. Configure routes to your LLM API (e.g., /v1/chat/completions → OpenAI).
  3. Add auth, rate limiting, and logging redaction.
  4. Use HTTPS (TLS) and store API keys securely.
  5. Paste your reverse-proxy URL into Janitor AI’s Proxy/Custom field and test.

8: Test your connection

  • Send a short prompt (“Say hello in one sentence”).
  • If it fails:
    • Use the Check API button (if available).
    • Re-paste the proxy URL and API key.
    • Try another model (some proxies default to specific engines).
    • Temporarily disable your forward proxy/VPN to isolate issues.

9: Tune your character for the new model

  1. Keep the system/persona concise but specific (tone, goals, boundaries).
  2. Add few-shot examples (1–3 mini exchanges).
  3. Test temperature/creativity if the proxy exposes model params.

10: Troubleshoot common proxy/LLM issues

  • 401/403 (Unauthorized): Wrong or missing API keys so re-add key; confirm it belongs to the target service.
  • 404/405 (Routing): Your reverse proxy path doesn’t match the LLM’s expected endpoint → fix route mapping.
  • 429 (Rate limit): Slow down. Add retry/backoff logic, add more proxies, or upgrade your plan. For no-code solutions to scaling without bans, see the no-code scrapers and Ziny setup guide.
  • TLS/handshake errors: Check HTTPS certs; ensure SNI/ALPN defaults; update proxy client.
  • High latency/timeouts: Switch proxy region; reduce request size; try a closer POP.

11: Security & compliance checklist

  • Never paste your personal LLM API keys into untrusted or unknown third-party proxies.
  • Prefer well-known providers; read privacy policies and logs/retention statements.
  • If you self-host, lock the server down (TLS, firewall, non-root user, secret storage).
  • Stay within the ToS of Janitor AI and the LLM provider (no abuse/bypass of prohibited use).

12: When to use proxies vs. native AP

  • Native API: Best for a straightforward experience, official vendor route, simple billing, nothing in the middle.
  • Reverse proxy: Best when you need one URL to act as a gateway across multiple models, with added filtering or team authentication.
  • Forward proxy: Best when your primary concern is privacy or accessing APIs blocked in your region. Particularly useful for running multi-account social media operations where IP separation matters.

Quick Reference (at a glance)

  • Janitor AI basics: Character platform + supports multiple LLM engines.
  • Why proxies matter: Privacy/geo-access (forward) and centralized model routing (reverse).
  • UI pointers (community): API settings → Proxy/Custom → “Other API/Proxy URL” + “Check API key.”

That is it. Follow the steps in order: native API first, then add a proxy (forward or reverse) once you know your base setup works. This keeps troubleshooting painless and gives you the flexibility to pick the best model + routing for your use case.

The post What is Janitor AI? Full Guide + Proxies appeared first on Ziny.io.

XLogin supports global websites and platforms and is applicable to all types of browser-based businesses, such as Google, Facebook, Twitter, Amazon, eBay, Etsy, TikTok, Tinder, PayPal, Stripe, Outlook, Gmail, and other online platforms.
